title: KISS decoder completes garbage frame after decode error
description: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/kiss_transport.c:104-110,119-124,140-145. After -EILSEQ (invalid escape) or -EOVERFLOW (oversized frame) the decoder sets in_frame=false and returns; the NEXT non-FEND byte hits the implicit-start path and becomes a fabricated CMD byte, remaining bytes of the malformed frame accumulate as DATA, and the malformed frame's own closing FEND completes and dispatches this garbage as a valid frame. Spurious frames reach AX.25/raw callbacks; a garbage cmd nibble can silently overwrite TxDelay/Persistence/SlotTime. spec/kiss-framing.md §1.1 frame definition; both reference impls discard until closing FEND (Rust test_reader_invalid_escape_does_not_stick, Python KissReader). Fix: on decode error enter discard-until-FEND state and clear has_cmd.
